what_it_does: Implements content search over an interim SQLite transcript index of Claude sessions, supports semantic (embedding via an external venv bridge) and lexical (term occurrence) modes, groups chunk hits by session, joins each hit to a live session registry entry at query time, and returns live handles containing session state, a matched point coordinate, a snippet, and runnable command templates.
observations: The module is explicitly pure-read: it never writes the index, spawns sessions, or posts fabric events.
Search modes are declared in a MODES registry with description, needs, and backing fields.
Semantic search depends on an embed-pplx health endpoint, an overlord venv python, and a chroma index; it fails loudly with teaching errors when any leg is missing.
Lexical search ranks by distinct query terms present, then total occurrences, with no tf-idf or model scoring.
mode='auto' chooses semantic when available and otherwise lexical, declaring mode_used in the result envelope.
Every envelope carries index stats: file count, chunk count, embedded chunk count, and coverage ratio.
Candidates are grouped by session_id so the caller gets one handle per session with the best chunk leading.
The point coordinate parses the exporter's turn-N-speaker anchor convention and includes turn, speaker, and timestamp hint.
Commands are concrete tool-call strings for describe, inspect, consult, gather, add_to_channel, and conditional deliver/wake/queue.
Unroutable corpus hits are kept by default with routable=false and the registry's own error message.
The caller-supplied suite object provides get_agent_session and AGENT_SESSION_STATES, but its interface is not defined here.
SQLite is opened read-only via URI mode=ro, and the vector query is delegated to a subprocess in the overlord venv.
standouts: Fail-loud, capability-honest design: missing prerequisites are named with remediation steps rather than returning empty results.
Live registry join at query time means returned state is current, not cached from index build.
Two very different search legs are normalized into the same candidate shape, then grouped into uniform live handles.
The result envelope carries both a soft point (quoted snippet in the message) and a hard point (structured coordinate for an R3.4 adapter).
conventions: Heavy spec-referencing docstrings (R4.2, R4.5, R11) frame design intent.
Environment-variable configurable paths with home-directory defaults.
Read-only database access via SQLite URI mode=ro.
Subprocess venv-bridge for dependencies deliberately excluded from this venv.
Early validation of closed vocabularies: mode, state, detail.
Registry-is-truth: live state overrides anything implied by the corpus.
concerns: Hard-coded default paths and service URLs may not exist outside the author's machine.
Semantic search uses a single 180-second subprocess call with no retry/backoff and surfaces only the last 500 characters of stderr.
Lexical scoring formula (distinct_terms * 1000 + occurrences) is arbitrary and may favor long chunks.
_handle calls suite.get_agent_session once per result with no batching.
Fallback session_id from Path(rel_path).stem is fragile if filenames differ from registry session ids.
_commands builds tool-call strings by string interpolation rather than a structured schema.
No pagination or cursor; grouping can discard lower-ranked sessions once k is reached.
urllib.request.urlopen health check does not explicitly guard against non-JSON responses.
notes: A read-only search adapter that turns transcript corpus hits into live, routable session handles with point coordinates and ready-to-run command templates.
questions: What is the exact contract of the suite object passed in (get_agent_session, AGENT_SESSION_STATES)?
What JSON schema does the wire script return for vector candidates?
What are the precise semantics and side effects of session_post verbs and channel_act actions?
How does the live registry distinguish states such as supervised-live versus closed?
Which caller is expected to execute the returned command strings—UI, MCP agent, or supervisor service?
Is there any concurrency control between read-only search queries and the reindexer writing the database?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it searches the corpus, joins to live registry state, and returns handles with point and commands, remaining a pure read layer.
apparent_themes: CQRS separation of read search from write session_post
live handles over static file paths
intention_signals: The file is trying to make session search a trustworthy, inspectable bridge between a throwaway transcript index and the live session fabric, so callers can locate relevant past conversations and immediately know whether and how they can act on them.
novelty: It explicitly surfaces unavailability as named teaching errors with remediation steps rather than silent emptiness, and it packages both a structured point coordinate and verbatim command strings into the same result envelope.
contribution: A deterministic, read-only search-to-handle join that exposes mode availability, index provenance, live registry state, and runnable next-step commands in a single envelope, keeping search pure while leaving action to callers.
summary_for_embedding: A Python module that performs semantic or lexical content search over a SQLite transcript index of Claude sessions, joins hits to live session registry state, and returns live handles with point coordinates and command templates.
intention_for_embedding: To close the search-to-handle chain so callers can locate relevant session content and immediately see whether and how to act on the matched session.

symbols.does: Checks whether semantic embedding search can run right now.
symbols.description: Validates three preconditions—overlord venv python, chroma index directory, and embed-pplx health endpoint—and returns an available flag plus a teaching reason naming the missing leg.

symbols.does: Opens a read-only SQLite connection to the substrate index.
symbols.description: Returns a URI mode=ro sqlite3.Row connection to DB_PATH; raises a ValueError with build instructions if the index file is missing.

symbols.does: Reports index coverage and counts for the envelope provenance block.
symbols.description: Queries the read-only database for file count, chunk count, and embedded chunk count under the claude-sessions vault, returning a coverage ratio so partial embedding is visible.

symbols.does: Normalizes a database row into a candidate dictionary.
symbols.description: Parses frontmatter JSON and builds a uniform candidate record with chunk_address, anchor, heading_path, text, rel_path, frontmatter dict, score, and reason.

symbols.does: Performs term search over chunk text with simple occurrence ranking.
symbols.description: Extracts query terms, scans chunks containing any term up to LEX_SCAN_CAP, scores by distinct terms present and total occurrences, then returns ranked candidates.

symbols.does: Performs embedding search via an external venv bridge.
symbols.description: Checks semantic availability, invokes the overlord-venv wire script as a subprocess to fetch vector candidates, resolves each candidate back to a chunk row by chunk_id or chunk_address, and returns cosine-scored candidates.

symbols.does: Parses a matched chunk anchor into a point coordinate.
symbols.description: Extracts turn number, speaker, and timestamp hint from the exporter's turn-N-speaker anchor convention and returns the coordinate object used by downstream adapters.

symbols.does: Builds launch-ready command strings for a matched session.
symbols.description: Returns a dictionary of concrete tool-call templates—describe, inspect, consult, gather, add_to_channel, plus conditional deliver/wake/queue—quoting a snippet in the message body.

symbols.does: Constructs one live handle from a corpus hit joined to registry state.
symbols.description: Fetches live session state from the provided suite, computes the primary verb based on state, assembles point/snippet/commands, and marks unroutable hits with the registry error.

symbols.does: Entry point that searches sessions and returns live handles.
symbols.description: Validates inputs, chooses semantic or lexical mode while declaring mode_used, queries chunks, groups by session, joins each to live registry state, filters by optional state and routability, and returns the envelope with index stats and availability.
